title: Grady Booch
body: Grady Booch (born February 27, 1955) is an American software engineer, best known for developing the Unified Modeling Language (UML) with Ivar Jacobson and James Rumbaugh. He is recognized internationally for his innovative work in software architecture, software engineering, and collaborative development environments.
categories: Fellows of the Association for Computing Machinery
United States Air Force Academy alumni
University of California, Santa Barbara alumni
